summary.intent: Address 3 P2 issues surfaced by Codex's second-pass review of the signature pad component: a stale Undo target, incomplete sentinel rejection in data-URL decoding, and a read-modify-write race in controlled mode.
summary.outcome: All 3 fixes implemented with TDD, tests green (74 ars-components + 3 ars-dom native + 5 wasm), clippy clean on native and wasm32 targets, specs synced, committed and pushed. Codex re-trigger queued for a third pass.

repo: Controlled-mode read-modify-write operations (DrawEnd, Undo, Clear, SyncProps-cancel) must build on `pending()` not `get()` — `get()` returns stale committed state before the parent round-trips, causing rapid strokes to overwrite each other.
Undo target must derive emptiness from post-pop point-based `is_empty()`, not `strokes.len()` — empty-stroke entries in the vec make len-based checks disagree with the point-level emptiness check.
The data-URL sentinel `data:,` must be rejected at the parse layer, not just at the zero-size canvas guard — oversized canvases also emit an empty payload and were not previously caught.
Native-testable pure helpers (e.g. `parse_data_url_header`) are the pattern for logic that runs in wasm at runtime but needs to be unit-tested on the native target.

code.finding: `parse_data_url_header` and `RasterFormat` import require `#[cfg(any(target_arch = "wasm32", test))]` gating — without it the native non-test lib build raises dead_code warnings that fail clippy CI.

All controlled-mode state mutation arms were reading from `get()` (committed state); replacing with `pending()` (working copy) is required to correctly accumulate multiple strokes before the parent acknowledges.

workflow: Codex multi-pass review loop: fix → TDD → clippy (native + wasm32) → xci-fast → commit/push → reply/resolve threads → re-trigger @codex. Each Codex pass can surface follow-on issues introduced by the prior round's fixes.
Running clippy separately for native and wasm32 targets catches distinct dead-code/unused-import issues that the other target masks — a single `cargo clippy` targeting one arch is insufficient.
xci-fast is run as a background task before committing to avoid blocking on the full CI suite while still gating commits on a fast integration check.

friction: Extracting `parse_data_url_header` into a shared pure helper triggered a native clippy dead_code error requiring a `#[cfg(any(target_arch = "wasm32", test))]` gate — needed two rounds of clippy fixes (first for the function, then for the `RasterFormat` import it dragged along).
The wasm-only nature of the decode path means cfg gating is required for any shared utility, adding boilerplate overhead when refactoring wasm logic into testable helpers.
open_items: Codex third-pass review not yet triggered at transcript end — loop continues until Codex gives a clean 👍 with no new threads.
PR reply/resolve for the 3 second-pass Codex threads was queued but not shown as completed in the transcript.
